message:
Add ast-grep tooling for pattern code organization (wa-4vx.5.6)

Enforces centralization of detection patterns in patterns.rs pack system:
- Rule: no-adhoc-regex - catches Regex::new() outside allowed modules
- Lint script with allowlist for patterns.rs, policy.rs, config.rs
- Self-test mode to verify rules catch violations
- Test fixtures demonstrating pass/fail cases

Usage: ./scripts/lint-patterns.sh --check (for CI)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -123,7 +123,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-4vx.5.3","title":"Implement initial built-in packs: core.codex/core.claude_code/core.gemini (+ core.wezterm stub)","description":"# Task: Implement initial built-in packs

## Goal
Ship the first stable rule set sufficient to drive v0.1 workflows.

## Required rules (minimum)
### Codex
- usage warnings (25/10/5)
- usage reached (retry time)
- token usage summary (total/input/cached/output/reasoning)
- resume hint (session id)
- device auth prompt (device code)

### Claude Code
- compaction anchor
- session banner (version/model) (best-effort)

### Gemini
- usage reached
- session summary (session id)
- model used

### WezTerm
- optional: mux/server diagnostics placeholders

## Deliverables
- Rule definitions with stable IDs and extraction regexes.
- Suggested remediation/workflow hints for:
  - handle_compaction
  - handle_usage_limits

## Testing
- Every rule must have at least one of:
  - a dedicated unit test, OR
  - a golden corpus fixture (`tests/corpus/**/*.txt` + `.expect.json`).

- Drift variants:
  - for each agent pack, include fixtures for at least one “format drift” variant (missing fields, reordered lines, extra banners).

- Safety:
  - extraction must never capture/store secrets; fixtures should include “secret-ish” strings to ensure redaction behavior is exercised.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every rule has unit tests or corpus fixtures.
- Rule IDs are stable and namespaced by pack.


## Rule IDs (v0; pinned)
This bead is the canonical place for the initial stable rule IDs (derived from PLAN.md Appendix C).

Per the naming scheme in `wa-4vx.5.1`, we use:
- `codex.*`
- `claude_code.*`
- `gemini.*`
- (`wezterm.*` reserved)

### Codex (`core.codex`)
| rule_id | anchors (fast path) | extraction (named) | event_type |
|--------|----------------------|--------------------|-----------|
| `codex.usage.warning_25` | `less than 25%` | `remaining`, `limit_hours` | `usage.warning` |
| `codex.usage.warning_10` | `less than 10%` | `remaining`, `limit_hours` | `usage.warning` |
| `codex.usage.warning_5` | `less than 5%` | `remaining`, `limit_hours` | `usage.warning` |
| `codex.usage.reached` | `You've hit your usage limit` | `try_again_at` | `usage.reached` |
| `codex.session.token_usage` | `Token usage:` | `total`, `input`, `cached`, `output`, `reasoning` | `session.summary` |
| `codex.session.resume_hint` | `codex resume` | `session_id` | `session.resume_hint` |
| `codex.auth.device_code_prompt` | `Enter this one-time code` | `code` | `auth.device_code` |

### Claude Code (`core.claude_code`)
| rule_id | anchors | extraction | event_type |
|--------|---------|------------|-----------|
| `claude_code.compaction` | `Conversation compacted` | none | `session.compaction` |
| `claude_code.banner` | `Claude Code v` | `version`, `model` (best-effort) | `session.start` |
| `claude_code.usage.warning` | (evolves) | (evolves) | `usage.warning` |
| `claude_code.usage.reached` | (evolves) | (evolves) | `usage.reached` |

### Gemini (`core.gemini`)
| rule_id | anchors | extraction | event_type |
|--------|---------|------------|-----------|
| `gemini.usage.reached` | `Usage limit reached for all Pro models` | none | `usage.reached` |
| `gemini.session.summary` | `Interaction Summary` | `session_id` | `session.summary` |
| `gemini.model.used` | `Responding with gemini-` | `model` | `session.model` |

### Naming note (PLAN vs wa conventions)
PLAN Appendix C.2 shows rule ids prefixed with `claude.*` (e.g., `claude.compaction`). The pack name, however, is `core.claude_code`.

To avoid ambiguity in our own namespaces, we standardize on `claude_code.*` internally.

Mapping (plan → canonical):
- `claude.compaction` → `claude_code.compaction`
- `claude.banner` → `claude_code.banner`
- `claude.usage.warning` → `claude_code.usage.warning`
- `claude.usage.reached` → `claude_code.usage.reached`

If we ever choose to support aliases, do it at the pack boundary (do not fork semantics).

### Notes / invariants
- IDs above are stable: we prefer improving match robustness (anchors/regex) while keeping IDs stable.
- If we must represent genuinely different semantics, add a new rule id rather than mutating an old one.
- Every rule above must have fixtures (positive + near-miss negative) and extraction validation.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.5.4","title":"State gating: require inferred agent match; avoid non-agent panes; segment-level dedup","description":"# Task: State gating + dedup

## Goal
Reduce false positives by incorporating context:
- only apply agent-specific rules when pane is inferred to be that agent
- or when the agent banner/anchor is present

## Why
Terminal output is noisy; strings like "Token usage" might appear in unrelated contexts.

## Deliverables
- Gate rules by:
  - inferred agent type (from pane registry)
  - optional additional anchors
- Dedup across segments:
  - prevent emitting the same detection repeatedly as tail windows overlap

## Testing
- Unit tests include:
  - known false positives in non-agent panes (must not fire)
  - dedupe window behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- Known false positives are suppressed.
- Detections are not spammed repeatedly for the same overlapping tail content.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.5.5","title":"Pattern tests: per-rule unit tests + golden corpus regression harness","description":"# Task: Pattern tests + golden corpus

## Goal
Make pattern drift obvious and easy to fix.

Patterns are the “sensory system” of wa; if they drift silently, automation becomes unreliable.

## Deliverables
- Unit tests for each extraction regex.
- Golden corpus under `tests/corpus/`:
  - input `.txt`
  - expected `.expect.json`
- Corpus runner:
  - run engine on each corpus file
  - compare actual vs expected detections
  - produce a clear per-fixture diff when mismatched

## Testing
- Must run as part of the normal `cargo test` suite (not “special CI only”).
- Runner behavior must be deterministic:
  - stable ordering of detections
  - stable JSON serialization
  - stable fixture iteration order
- Add a “how to add a fixture” checklist:
  1) capture real output
  2) drop into `tests/corpus/<agent>/...`
  3) run tests to see the diff
  4) update pack/rule until green

## Logging / diagnostics
- When a corpus fixture fails:
  - print the rule id(s) involved
  - show a minimal excerpt/snippet location (bounded)
  - avoid dumping entire pane contents

## Acceptance Criteria
- Adding a new real-world capture yields:
  - a new failing fixture
  - an updated rule
  - passing tests
- Corpus runner output is actionable (failure points to the exact fixture + rule id).

-{"id":"wa-4vx.5.6","title":"ast-grep tooling: enforce pattern code organization + ban ad-hoc regex usage","description":"# Task: ast-grep tooling for pattern development (codebase lints)

## Goal
Use `ast-grep` to enforce *structural* codebase constraints that keep the detection system maintainable:
- rule definitions live in the pattern system (packs / dedicated module)
- ad-hoc regex usage for detection is disallowed outside the pattern engine
- tests/fixtures exist for rules and for any code that defines patterns

This implements the “Rule development tooling” part of PLAN.md §6.4 ast-grep Integration.

## Why
Pattern drift is inevitable. What kills projects is when:
- patterns are sprinkled throughout the codebase
- regex logic grows “organically” without tests
- contributors add quick hacks to fix one drift case and accidentally increase false positives

A structure-aware lint is the fastest way to prevent long-term entropy.

## Deliverables
- An `ast-grep` ruleset (committed with the repo) that enforces:
  1) **No ad-hoc detection regex in non-pattern modules**
     - Example: forbid `Regex::new("...")` in most modules except the pattern engine / pack loader.

  2) **Pattern definitions centralized**
     - Any new pattern rule must be added via the pack system (`wa-4vx.5.1`) rather than inline matching.

  3) **Tests/fixtures exist**
     - When rule definitions are modified, ensure there is at least one:
       - unit test, or
       - golden corpus fixture

- A documented “how to run” workflow for contributors:
  - `ast-grep` invocations and how to interpret failures

- Optional CI integration (recommended):
  - run the ast-grep ruleset in CI for changed Rust files

## Testing
- Meta-tests for the lints themselves:
  - include small fixture Rust snippets that should pass
  - include small fixture Rust snippets that should fail

- CI test:
  - ensure the ast-grep tool is invoked and failures are actionable

## Acceptance Criteria
- A contributor cannot accidentally add new detection regex logic outside the pattern system without CI catching it.
- Lint failures point to a precise file:line:col with an actionable message.

","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:40:38.336853336Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-22T07:49:30.229235288Z","compaction_level":0,"labels":["area-patterns","area-tests","area-tooling","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.5.6","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:40:44.097453235Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.5.6","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:40:54.866133228Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":33,"issue_id":"wa-4vx.5.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Complete

### Deliverables

1. **ast-grep configuration** (sgconfig.yml):
   - Points to rules/ directory for rule definitions

2. **Rule: no-adhoc-regex** (rules/no-adhoc-regex.yml):
   - Detects Regex::new() calls in Rust code
   - Provides actionable error message and fix suggestions
   - Links to allowed files and proper pattern system usage

3. **Lint script** (scripts/lint-patterns.sh):
   - Applies file allowlist (patterns.rs, policy.rs, config.rs)
   - Also allows test/bench files for testing purposes
   - --check mode for CI integration
   - --self-test mode to verify rules work correctly
   - Clear violation output with remediation hints

4. **Test fixtures** (rules/tests/):
   - should_fail_adhoc_regex.rs - demonstrates violations
   - should_pass_in_patterns.rs - shows allowed usage

### Usage

```bash
# Lint all Rust files
./scripts/lint-patterns.sh

# CI mode (exit 1 on violations)
./scripts/lint-patterns.sh --check

# Verify rules work
./scripts/lint-patterns.sh --self-test
```

### Allowlist Rationale

- **patterns.rs**: Pattern detection engine - this is WHERE patterns live
- **policy.rs**: Command safety gating and secret detection (different domain)
- **config.rs**: Configuration parsing with user-defined patterns","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:49:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-4vx.6","title":"[EPIC] Watcher daemon runtime (observe loop + subscriptions)","description":"# Watcher daemon runtime

## Goal
Provide the long-running `wa watch` process that ties together:
- pane discovery + ingest tailers
- storage writer
- pattern detection
- event feeds (robot/MCP subscriptions)

while maintaining the critical split:
- Observation loop never mutates terminal state
- Action loop (workflows) is separate and guarded

## Why this exists
Most value comes from continuous monitoring. The daemon is the “always-on nervous system”.

## Deliverables
- `wa watch` command that:
  - starts ingest pipeline
  - persists segments/events
  - runs pattern engine
  - optionally emits metrics and health snapshots
- subscription surfaces:
  - robot/MCP can read state/events from wa without re-polling WezTerm
- lifecycle:
  - clean shutdown with DB flush
  - restart safety (no corrupt state)

## Acceptance
- `wa watch` runs for hours without leak or drift.
- Idle overhead is low due to adaptive polling.
- Health output shows queue depth + lag + DB write status.



## Success Criteria
- `wa watch` runs as a single-instance, workspace-scoped daemon (lock enforced) with graceful shutdown.
- Observation pipeline runs end-to-end: discover panes → tail → persist → detect → persist events.
- IPC lane (user-var events) delivers signals reliably to the watcher.
- Health/metrics/logging make failures diagnosable; crash reports are redacted and actionable.
- Integration tests validate runtime wiring without requiring a real WezTerm session; E2E validates with a real mux server.


## Testing
- Integration tests (synthetic):
  - Drive the runtime with deterministic delta fixtures (no WezTerm required) and assert:
    - segments/events persisted
    - health snapshot reports expected queue depths/lag
    - graceful shutdown flushes writer queue
  - Include “crash/panic path” tests to ensure crash report generation is redacted and bounded.

- E2E tests (real-ish):
  - At least one runner scenario exercises a real `wa watch` lifecycle (start → ingest → stop → restart) and validates artifacts (`wa-4vx.10.15`, `wa-4vx.10.21`).

- Logging observability tests:
  - Verify per-pane correlation IDs and structured fields exist and can be used to reconstruct a workflow failure from logs alone.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.6.1","title":"Implement `wa watch` CLI command (args, lifecycle, graceful shutdown)","description":"# Task: Implement wa watch CLI command

## Goal
Provide the entry point for the long-running watcher/daemon.

This is the command users run to "turn wa on".

## Workspace + config UX (critical)
wa watch must not make users guess where state lives.

- Accept workspace selection:
  - --workspace <path>
  - WA_WORKSPACE
- Accept config selection:
  - --config <path> (optional override)
  - otherwise load from OS-default config location
- Always print (in verbose logs and/or startup banner) the resolved paths:
  - workspace root
  - DB path
  - lock path
  - IPC socket path
  - log file path (if enabled)

## Requirements
- Starts the observation runtime.
- Acquires the workspace single-instance lock before doing any work.
- Supports safe shutdown:
  - stops ingest
  - drains channels
  - flushes DB writer queue
  - closes IPC endpoints
  - releases lock

## Flags (v0)
- poll interval override
- enable/disable pattern detection
- (Phase 2) enable workflows / auto-handle
- (Phase 4) metrics bind
- logging verbosity / log-to-file toggle

## Testing
- Integration tests:
  - start/stop lifecycle acquires/releases lock deterministically
  - graceful shutdown flushes storage writer queue
  - restart in same workspace succeeds (no stale lock)
- E2E:
  - `wa-4vx.10.7` (capture+FTS), `wa-4vx.10.15` (graceful shutdown), `wa-4vx.10.21` (wa stop), and related cases exercise wa watch under real mux

## Acceptance Criteria
- wa watch uses the shared config loader and workspace resolution (no ad-hoc paths).
- wa watch can be stopped cleanly and exits quickly even under load.
- After shutdown, restarting wa watch in the same workspace works (lock released, DB usable).

Prints resolved paths on startup. Creates storage, pattern engine, and ObservationRuntime. Handles SIGINT/SIGTERM for graceful shutdown.","compaction_level":0,"labels":["area-daemon","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.6.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T08:58:02.826629594Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.6.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-18T08:57:52.263878318Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.6.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T11:00:50.041202463Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-4vx.6.2","title":"Wire runtime: start ingest → persist segments → run PatternEngine → persist detections","description":"# Task: Wire the observation runtime

## Goal
Connect the major passive subsystems:
- WezTerm interface → ingest pipeline
- ingest deltas → storage segments
- ingest deltas → pattern engine → detection events
- detection events → storage events

## Why
This is the core “sense” loop that gives wa value even before workflows exist.

## Deliverables
- Task supervisor that starts:
  - StorageHandle
  - discovery + tailers
  - pattern detection consumer
- Bounded channels between components.
- Explicitly enforce that the observation loop never calls any send/act APIs.

## Testing
- Integration tests using a synthetic delta source (no WezTerm required) should validate:
  - segments persisted
  - detections emitted
  - events persisted
- E2E coverage:
  - `wa-4vx.10.7`

## Acceptance Criteria
- With a live WezTerm session:
  - output segments are written
  - detections appear for fixtures when output contains patterns
  - events can be queried via robot/human surfaces
- The observation runtime does not perform any input injection actions.

@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+# Rule: Ban ad-hoc Regex::new usage outside pattern/policy/config modules
+#
+# Detection regex should live in patterns.rs (via PatternPack/RuleDef).
+# Safety regex for command gating and secret detection lives in policy.rs.
+# Configuration pattern matching lives in config.rs.
+#
+# Adding regex elsewhere risks:
+# - Pattern drift and duplication
+# - Missing tests/corpus entries
+# - Inconsistent detection behavior
+
+id: no-adhoc-fancy-regex
+language: rust
+severity: error
+message: "Ad-hoc Regex::new() usage outside allowed modules. Detection patterns should use the pattern pack system in patterns.rs. Safety/secret patterns belong in policy.rs. Config matching belongs in config.rs."
+
+rule:
+  kind: call_expression
+  pattern: Regex::new($PATTERN)
+  # This matches `Regex::new(...)` calls
+
+# We use ast-grep's file filtering at scan time via script
+# Since ast-grep's built-in file filtering is limited,
+# the lint script will apply the allowlist
+
+note: |
+  Allowed files for Regex::new:
+  - crates/wa-core/src/patterns.rs (pattern detection engine)
+  - crates/wa-core/src/policy.rs (command safety, secret detection)
+  - crates/wa-core/src/config.rs (configuration parsing)
+
+  If you need detection for agent state transitions, add a RuleDef to the appropriate PatternPack in patterns.rs.
+
+fix: |
+  // Add detection via pattern pack system:
+  // 1. Add to patterns.rs as a RuleDef with anchors + optional regex
+  // 2. Include in appropriate PatternPack (core.codex, core.claude_code, etc.)
+  // 3. Add corpus test fixture

@@ -0,0 +1,250 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# lint-patterns.sh - Enforce pattern code organization via ast-grep
+#
+# This script enforces:
+# 1. No ad-hoc Regex::new() outside allowed modules
+# 2. Detection patterns centralized in pattern packs
+#
+# Usage:
+#   ./scripts/lint-patterns.sh             # Lint all Rust files
+#   ./scripts/lint-patterns.sh --check     # Check mode (exit 1 on violations)
+#   ./scripts/lint-patterns.sh --self-test # Verify lint rules work correctly
+#   ./scripts/lint-patterns.sh --help      # Show help
+#
+# Allowed files for Regex::new:
+#   - crates/wa-core/src/patterns.rs  (pattern detection engine)
+#   - crates/wa-core/src/policy.rs    (command safety, secret detection)
+#   - crates/wa-core/src/config.rs    (configuration parsing)
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Colors for output
+RED='\033[0;31m'
+GREEN='\033[0;32m'
+YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
+NC='\033[0m' # No Color
+
+# Allowed files for Regex::new usage
+ALLOWED_REGEX_FILES=(
+    "crates/wa-core/src/patterns.rs"
+    "crates/wa-core/src/policy.rs"
+    "crates/wa-core/src/config.rs"
+)
+
+# Test fixtures are allowed to use regex for testing purposes
+ALLOWED_REGEX_PATTERNS=(
+    "*/tests/*.rs"
+    "*/benches/*.rs"
+    "*_test.rs"
+)
+
+usage() {
+    echo "Usage: $0 [--check] [--self-test] [--help] [FILES...]"
+    echo ""
+    echo "Lint Rust files for pattern code organization violations."
+    echo ""
+    echo "Options:"
+    echo "  --check      Check mode - exit with error if violations found"
+    echo "  --self-test  Verify lint rules work by checking test fixtures"
+    echo "  --help       Show this help message"
+    echo "  FILES...     Specific files to lint (default: all *.rs in crates/)"
+    echo ""
+    echo "Allowed files for Regex::new:"
+    for f in "${ALLOWED_REGEX_FILES[@]}"; do
+        echo "  - $f"
+    done
+}
+
+# Self-test function: verify rules catch violations
+run_self_test() {
+    echo "Running lint rule self-tests..."
+    echo ""
+
+    local passed=0
+    local failed=0
+
+    # Test 1: should_fail fixture must be caught
+    echo "Test 1: Verifying bad fixture triggers violation..."
+    local bad_fixture="rules/tests/should_fail_adhoc_regex.rs"
+    if [[ -f "$bad_fixture" ]]; then
+        local output
+        output=$(ast-grep scan --rule rules/no-adhoc-regex.yml "$bad_fixture" 2>&1 || true)
+        if echo "$output" | grep -q "error\[no-adhoc"; then
+            echo -e "  ${GREEN}PASS${NC}: Bad fixture correctly flagged"
+            passed=$((passed + 1))
+        else
+            echo -e "  ${RED}FAIL${NC}: Bad fixture not caught by lint"
+            failed=$((failed + 1))
+        fi
+    else
+        echo -e "  ${YELLOW}SKIP${NC}: Bad fixture not found: $bad_fixture"
+    fi
+
+    # Test 2: allowed files should not be checked (via script filtering)
+    echo "Test 2: Verifying allowlist filtering works..."
+    if is_allowed_regex_file "crates/wa-core/src/patterns.rs"; then
+        echo -e "  ${GREEN}PASS${NC}: patterns.rs correctly in allowlist"
+        ((passed++))
+    else
+        echo -e "  ${RED}FAIL${NC}: patterns.rs not in allowlist"
+        ((failed++))
+    fi
+
+    if is_allowed_regex_file "crates/wa-core/src/policy.rs"; then
+        echo -e "  ${GREEN}PASS${NC}: policy.rs correctly in allowlist"
+        ((passed++))
+    else
+        echo -e "  ${RED}FAIL${NC}: policy.rs not in allowlist"
+        ((failed++))
+    fi
+
+    # Test 3: non-allowed file should not be in allowlist
+    echo "Test 3: Verifying non-allowed files are checked..."
+    if ! is_allowed_regex_file "crates/wa-core/src/workflows.rs"; then
+        echo -e "  ${GREEN}PASS${NC}: workflows.rs correctly not in allowlist"
+        ((passed++))
+    else
+        echo -e "  ${RED}FAIL${NC}: workflows.rs incorrectly in allowlist"
+        ((failed++))
+    fi
+
+    echo ""
+    echo "----------------------------------------"
+    echo "Self-test results: ${passed} passed, ${failed} failed"
+
+    if [[ $failed -gt 0 ]]; then
+        return 1
+    fi
+    return 0
+}
+
+# Check if file is in allowed list
+is_allowed_regex_file() {
+    local file="$1"
+
+    # Check exact matches
+    for allowed in "${ALLOWED_REGEX_FILES[@]}"; do
+        if [[ "$file" == *"$allowed" ]]; then
+            return 0
+        fi
+    done
+
+    # Check patterns (tests, benches)
+    for pattern in "${ALLOWED_REGEX_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
+        # shellcheck disable=SC2053
+        if [[ "$file" == $pattern ]]; then
+            return 0
+        fi
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+# Main lint function
+lint_files() {
+    local check_mode="${1:-false}"
+    shift || true
+    local files=("$@")
+
+    local violations=0
+    local checked=0
+
+    # If no files specified, find all Rust files in crates
+    if [[ ${#files[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
+        mapfile -t files < <(find crates -name "*.rs" -type f 2>/dev/null || true)
+    fi
+
+    # Check for ast-grep
+    if ! command -v ast-grep &> /dev/null; then
+        echo -e "${RED}Error: ast-grep not found. Install with: cargo install ast-grep${NC}"
+        exit 1
+    fi
+
+    # Filter out allowed files for regex check
+    local files_to_check=()
+    for file in "${files[@]}"; do
+        if ! is_allowed_regex_file "$file"; then
+            files_to_check+=("$file")
+        fi
+    done
+
+    if [[ ${#files_to_check[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
+        echo -e "${GREEN}No files to check (all files in allowlist)${NC}"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    echo "Checking ${#files_to_check[@]} files for pattern violations..."
+    echo ""
+
+    # Run ast-grep on each file
+    for file in "${files_to_check[@]}"; do
+        if [[ ! -f "$file" ]]; then
+            continue
+        fi
+
+        checked=$((checked + 1))
+
+        # Run ast-grep and capture output
+        local output
+        output=$(ast-grep scan --rule rules/no-adhoc-regex.yml "$file" 2>&1 || true)
+
+        if [[ -n "$output" ]] && [[ "$output" != *"0 error"* ]]; then
+            violations=$((violations + 1))
+            echo -e "${RED}Violation in: $file${NC}"
+            echo "$output"
+            echo ""
+        fi
+    done
+
+    echo "----------------------------------------"
+    echo -e "Checked: ${checked} files"
+
+    if [[ $violations -gt 0 ]]; then
+        echo -e "${RED}Violations: ${violations}${NC}"
+        echo ""
+        echo "Fix:"
+        echo "  - Move detection patterns to patterns.rs using RuleDef/PatternPack"
+        echo "  - Or add file to allowlist if this is a legitimate use case"
+
+        if [[ "$check_mode" == "true" ]]; then
+            return 1
+        fi
+    else
+        echo -e "${GREEN}No violations found${NC}"
+    fi
+
+    return 0
+}
+
+# Parse arguments
+check_mode=false
+self_test=false
+files=()
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --check)
+            check_mode=true
+            shift
+            ;;
+        --self-test)
+            self_test=true
+            shift
+            ;;
+        --help|-h)
+            usage
+            exit 0
+            ;;
+        *)
+            files+=("$1")
+            shift
+            ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+# Run self-test or linting
+if [[ "$self_test" == "true" ]]; then
+    run_self_test
+else
+    lint_files "$check_mode" "${files[@]}"
+fi

@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+# ast-grep configuration for wezterm_automata
+# Enforces pattern code organization and bans ad-hoc regex usage
+#
+# Run: ast-grep scan --rule rules/
+# Or:  ./scripts/lint-patterns.sh
+
+ruleDirs:
+  - rules
